analysis.consistency: The spell description is internally consistent. Effects clearly relate to controlling freestanding water within a defined cubic area and can be switched or maintained on subsequent turns.
analysis.quality: High. The spell provides four distinct, thematic, and mechanically sound options for controlling water, scaling the complexity based on the chosen effect (utility like flooding/redirecting, or combat/control like parting water or creating a whirlpool). The component cost (V, S, M) and duration (Concentration) are appropriate for a 4th-level spell.

effects_analysis.name: Flood
effects_analysis.mechanic: Area denial/environmental manipulation. Rising water level (20ft) or a repeating wave (20ft tall) on large bodies of water. The wave mechanism introduces a small degree of randomness (25% chance to capsize Huge or smaller vehicles), adding narrative impact.

effects_analysis.name: Part Water
effects_analysis.mechanic: Environmental control/creation of temporary terrain (a trench). Provides temporary space/barrier, reversing automatically over one round, which is a reasonable duration for the effect.
effects_analysis.notes: The slow restoration prevents permanent map alteration.

effects_analysis.name: Redirect Flow
effects_analysis.mechanic: Movement control for existing currents. Highly situational but powerful utility for diverting rivers or streams around obstacles.
effects_analysis.notes: Effect is constrained to the spell area after which normal physics resume.

effects_analysis.name: Whirlpool
effects_analysis.mechanic: Direct combat effect. Requires specific initial water conditions (50x25ft minimum). Creates sustained area control (pulling creatures toward the center via a contested check, or forced save if entering). Damage (2d8 bludgeoning) is reasonable for the level, and being 'caught' requires an action to escape against a check with disadvantage.
effects_analysis.notes: The difficulty checks (spell save DC) and damage scaling seem balanced for a 4th-level concentration spell.

mechanics.balance_assessment: The spell is well-balanced for a 4th-level slot. It offers powerful area manipulation (Flood, Part Water) or direct combat control (Whirlpool) dependent on the caster's choice and environment. The action economy (Action to cast, Action on subsequent turns to alter) is fair.

analysis.archivist_notes: Data is complete and accurately formatted according to standard D&D 5e conventions. No normalization or correction required.
